description_long: This object consists of several slender, green stems rising vertically, each adorned with narrow, lanceolate leaves and topped with delicate, pale blue to white flowers. The flowers are small, with five distinct petals arranged radially around a central disk, and are distributed at the tips of the stems. Numerous unopened buds are visible along the upper portions of the stems, indicating various stages of blooming. The stems are thin and flexible, with a slightly rough, matte surface, and the leaves are sparse and linear, hugging the stem closely. The overall structure is airy and open, allowing light to pass through the spaces between stems and flowers. This plant is typical of ornamental or wildflowers found in urban parks, roadside verges, or landscaped gardens.
description: A cluster of slender green stems with narrow leaves and delicate, pale blue to white five-petaled flowers at the tips. The plant has an airy, open structure with visible buds and a natural, wildflower appearance.
description_view_0: Two main stems with several pale blue flowers and buds are visible, showing the plant's open, vertical structure.
description_view_1: The stems appear more closely grouped, with flowers and buds facing slightly left, highlighting the density of the upper foliage.
description_view_2: The flowers are more evenly spaced, and the stems are seen from a different angle, emphasizing the radial arrangement of the petals.
description_view_3: The plant appears more spread out, with flowers and buds facing outward, showing the airy distribution of blooms.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, with five distinct petals per flower forming a symmetrical, star-like pattern around the central disk. The petals do not overlap significantly and are evenly spaced, typical of many wildflowers.
